context: Tests whether the agent correctly applies scope in commit messages — adding it when a change is confined to one module, omitting it when the change is cross-cutting, and formatting the scope name in lowercase with hyphens.

checklist.name: api scope on rate limiting
checklist.description: The API rate limiting commit includes a scope derived from the module name (e.g., 'api')

checklist.name: auth scope on bcrypt change
checklist.description: The password hashing upgrade commit includes a scope that references the auth module (e.g., 'auth')

checklist.name: db scope on pool fix
checklist.description: The connection pool bug fix includes a scope that references the db module (e.g., 'db')

checklist.name: user-profile scope on new endpoint
checklist.description: The new activity history endpoint commit includes a scope referencing the user-profile module (e.g., 'user-profile')

checklist.name: No scope on cross-cutting change
checklist.description: The ESLint/Prettier/.editorconfig commit does NOT include a scope, since it spans multiple modules

checklist.name: Scope lowercase
checklist.description: All scope values that are present use only lowercase characters (no uppercase letters)

checklist.name: Scope hyphenated not camelCase
checklist.description: Any multi-word scope (e.g., user-profile) uses hyphens rather than camelCase or underscores

checklist.name: Subject line length
checklist.description: All five subject lines (first line of each commit message) are 72 characters or fewer
